---
title: "OmopSketch"
subtitle: "Characterise databases"
format:
  revealjs: 
    theme: [simple, ../../styleSS25.scss]
    incremental: true   
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    preview-links: auto
    margin: 0.07
    code-link: true
    code-line-numbers: false
    height: 900
    width: 1600
    footer: |
      <div style="position: relative; width: 100%;">
        <div style="text-align: center; font-weight: 500;">Real World Evidence Summer School 2026</div>
        <div style="position: fixed; top: 20px; right: 20px;"><a href="../index.qmd" style="text-decoration: none;">⬅ Back to Presentations</a></div>
      </div>
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: true
editor: visual
---

```{r, echo=FALSE}
options(width = 120)
```

## Overview and motivation

-   OmopSketch is designed to **summarise key information** of an OMOP-mapped database.
-   Two main use cases:
    -   **General characterisation** — understand what's in your database
    -   **Feasibility assessment** — is this database suitable for my study?
-   Available on [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/OmopSketch/index.html) and [GitHub](https://github.com/OHDSI/OmopSketch).

. . .

<center><img src="https://github.com/OHDSI/OmopSketch/blob/main/man/figures/logo.png?raw=true" alt="OmopSketch logo" width="200"/></center>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Setup

## Load packages and create the CDM object

-   Load the required packages

. . .

```{r}
library(OmopSketch)
library(CDMConnector)
library(duckdb)
library(dplyr)
library(here)
library(omock)
```

. . .

-   Create a CDM object from the **delphi-100k** synthetic dataset

. . .

```{r, eval=FALSE}
con <- dbConnect(drv = duckdb(dbdir = here("Databases", "delphi.duckdb")))

cdm <- cdmFromCon(
  con = con,
  cdmSchema = "main",
  writeSchema = "results",
  writePrefix = "os_"
)
```


```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
cdm <- mockCdmFromDataset(datasetName = "delphi-100k_5.4", source = "duckdb")
```

. . .

-   The `cdm` object is your gateway to all OmopSketch functions — every function takes `cdm` as its first argument.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Snapshot

## What is a snapshot?

`summariseOmopSnapshot()` captures **metadata about the CDM at a point in time** — useful for reproducibility and maintaining clear records of changes.

. . .

### Information provided:

-   **Vocabulary version** — from the `vocabulary` table
-   **Table sizes** — record counts for `person` and `observation_period`
-   **Observation period span** — earliest and latest dates in the data
-   **Source type** — origin or type of the data source

## `summariseOmopSnapshot()`

-   Returns a [summarised result](https://darwin-eu-dev.github.io/omopgenerics/articles/summarised_result.html) object.

. . .

```{r, warning=FALSE}
snapshot <- summariseOmopSnapshot(cdm = cdm)

glimpse(snapshot)
```

## `tableOmopSnapshot()`

-   Format the result into a [gt](https://gt.rstudio.com/) table

. . .

::: {style="overflow-y: scroll; height: 650px;"}
```{r, warning=FALSE}
tableOmopSnapshot(result = snapshot, type = "gt")
```
:::

# 2. Observation period

## What does the observation period tell us?

`summariseObservationPeriod()` summarises the **observation_period** table.

. . .

### Information provided:

-   **Number of records per person** — how many observation periods per individual
-   **Duration in days** — length of each observation period
-   **Days to next observation period** — gaps between consecutive periods
-   **Data quality** (optional) — flags inconsistencies
-   **Missingness** (optional) — counts of missing values per column

## `summariseObservationPeriod()`

. . .

```{r, warning=FALSE}
obs_result <- summariseObservationPeriod(cdm, missingData = TRUE, quality = TRUE)
glimpse(obs_result)
```

## `tableObservationPeriod()`

. . .

::: {style="overflow-y: scroll; height: 650px;"}
```{r, warning=FALSE}
tableObservationPeriod(result = obs_result, type = "gt")
```
:::

## `plotObservationPeriod()` — Number of subjects

. . .

```{r, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
plotObservationPeriod(
  result = obs_result,
  plotType = "barplot",
  variableName = "Number subjects"
)
```

## `plotObservationPeriod()` — Duration in days

. . .

```{r, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
plotObservationPeriod(
  result = obs_result,
  plotType = "densityplot",
  variableName = "Duration in days",
  facet = "observation_period_ordinal"
)
```

# 3. Clinical tables

## What does clinical table characterisation do?

`summariseClinicalRecords()` provides a **comprehensive audit** of any clinical table in the CDM.

. . .

### Information provided:

-   **General information**
    -   Records & subjects — total counts
    -   Records per person — mean, SD, median, IQR, min, max
-   **Concept summary** (`conceptSummary = TRUE`)
    -   Domains / Concept type / Vocabularies / Standard concepts
-   **Missingness** (`missingData = TRUE`)
-   **Quality checks** (`quality = TRUE`)
    -   \% records in observation period
    -   Records with end before start or start before birth date

## `summariseClinicalRecords()`

. . .

```{r, warning=FALSE}
clinical_result <- summariseClinicalRecords(
  cdm            = cdm, 
  omopTableName  = "condition_occurrence", 
  conceptSummary = TRUE,
  missingData    = TRUE,
  quality        = TRUE
)
```

. . .

-   You can pass multiple tables at once: `omopTableName = c("condition_occurrence", "drug_exposure")`

## `tableClinicalRecords()`

. . .

::: {style="overflow-y: scroll; height: 650px;"}
```{r, warning=FALSE}
tableClinicalRecords(result = clinical_result, type = "gt")
```
:::

## ✏️ Your turn: Clinical table audit {background-color="#f0f4ff"}

**Task:** Characterise **both** `drug_exposure` and `procedure_occurrence` in one call. Include missing data and quality checks.

. . .

Then answer:

1.  Which table has more records per person on average?
2.  What % of drug records are within the observation period?

. . .

<details>

<summary>💡 Click to see solution</summary>

```{r, eval=FALSE}
result_ex <- summariseClinicalRecords(
  cdm           = cdm,
  omopTableName = c("drug_exposure", "procedure_occurrence"),
  missingData   = TRUE,
  quality       = TRUE
)

tableClinicalRecords(result = result_ex, type = "gt")
```

</details>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 4. Trends over time

## What do trends tell us?

-   `summariseTrend()` reveals **how database content changes over time** — essential for spotting data gaps, coverage periods, and temporal changes.

## `event` vs `episode`

. . .

`summariseTrend()` has two separate arguments for tables — and the choice changes **how records are counted across time intervals**.

. . .

**`event`** — tables where occurrences happen at a **single point in time**

-   Each record is assigned to the interval containing its **start date only**
-   e.g. a diagnosis on 3 March 2015 → counts in 2015, nowhere else
-   Typical tables: `condition_occurrence`, `drug_exposure`, `procedure_occurrence`, `measurement`, `observation`, `visit_occurrence`, `death`

. . .

**`episode`** — tables that describe **periods spanning over time**

-   Each record contributes to **every interval between its start and end date**
-   e.g. an observation period from Jan 2015 to Dec 2016 → counts in both 2015 and 2016
-   Typical tables: `observation_period`, `drug_era`, `condition_era`

## Outputs available

. . .

| `output` argument | What it counts                       |
|-------------------|--------------------------------------|
| `"record"`        | Number of records (default)          |
| `"person"`        | Number of distinct persons           |
| `"person-days"`   | Number of person-days (episode only) |
| `"sex"`           | Number of females                    |
| `"age"`           | Median age at start of period        |

## `summariseTrend()`

. . .

```{r, warning=FALSE}
trend_result <- summariseTrend(
  cdm       = cdm,
  event     = c("condition_occurrence", "drug_exposure"),
  interval  = "years",   # "years" | "quarters" | "months" | "overall"
  output    = "record",  # "record" | "person" | "person-days" | "sex" | "age"
  dateRange = as.Date(c("1950-01-01", "2000-12-31"))
)

glimpse(trend_result)
```

## `summariseTrend()` — check event/episode type

. . .

> You can check how each table was treated in the result settings. The `type` column will show `"event"` or `"episode"` per table.

. . .

```{r, warning=FALSE}
settings(trend_result) |> glimpse()
```

## `plotTrend()`

. . .

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
plotTrend(result = trend_result, facet = "omop_table", colour = "cdm_name")
```

## `tableTrend()`

. . .

::: {style="overflow-y: scroll; height: 650px;"}
```{r, warning=FALSE}
tableTrend(result = trend_result, type = "reactable")
```
:::

## ✏️ Your turn: Sex-stratified trends {background-color="#f0f4ff"}

**Task:** Create a plot showing the **yearly** trend in records for `drug_exposure` and `condition_occurrence`, stratified by **sex**, for the date range **1990–2010**.

. . .

Hints:

-   `output = "record"` gives you record counts
-   `sex = TRUE` gives you sex-stratified summaries
-   `dateRange = as.Date(c("1990-01-01", "2010-12-31"))`
-   Use `facet = "omop_table"` and `colour = "sex"` in the plot

. . .

<details>

<summary>💡 Click to see solution</summary>

```{r, eval=FALSE}
result_sex <- summariseTrend(
  cdm       = cdm,
  event     = c("drug_exposure", "condition_occurrence"),
  interval  = "years",
  output    = "record",
  sex       = TRUE,
  dateRange = as.Date(c("1990-01-01", "2010-12-31"))
)

plotTrend(result = result_sex, facet = "omop_table", colour = "sex")
```

</details>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 5. Concept counts

## `summariseConceptIdCounts()`

-   Counts how often each concept ID appears — useful for **feasibility** (do we have enough patients with condition X?).

. . .

```{r, warning=FALSE}
concept_result <- summariseConceptIdCounts(
  cdm           = cdm, 
  omopTableName = "procedure_occurrence",
  countBy       = c("record", "person")
)
glimpse(concept_result)
```

## `tableConceptIdCounts()`

-   Filter using the `display` argument:

. . .

| `display`             | What is shown                         |
|-----------------------|---------------------------------------|
| `"overall"` (default) | All records                           |
| `"standard"`          | Standard concepts only                |
| `"source"`            | Source codes only                     |
| `"missing standard"`  | Source codes with no standard mapping |
| `"missing source"`    | Standard concepts with no source code |

## `tableConceptIdCounts()`

. . .

```{r, warning=FALSE}
tableConceptIdCounts(result = concept_result, display = "standard", type = "reactable")
```

## `tableTopConceptCounts()`

. . .

```{r, warning=FALSE}
tableTopConceptCounts(result = concept_result, countBy = "record", top = 10, type = "gt")
```

# 6. Full database characterisation

## `databaseCharacteristics()`

-   `databaseCharacteristics()` runs **all summaries in one call**.

. . .

```{r, warning=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
full_result <- databaseCharacteristics(
  cdm             = cdm,
  omopTableName   = c("drug_exposure", "condition_occurrence"),
  sex             = TRUE,
  ageGroup        = list(c(0, 50), c(51, Inf)),
  interval        = "years",
  dateRange       = as.Date(c("2010-01-01", "2020-12-31")),
  conceptIdCounts = FALSE
)
```

## `shinyCharacteristics()`

-   Then launch a **Shiny app** to explore everything interactively:

. . .

```{r, warning=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
shinyCharacteristics(result = full_result, directory = here())
```

## Live Shiny demo

. . .

```{=html}
<iframe src="https://dpa-pde-oxford.shinyapps.io/OmopSketchCharacterisation/"
        width="100%"
        height="600"
        style="border: none;">
</iframe>
```

[↗ Open full app](https://dpa-pde-oxford.shinyapps.io/OmopSketchCharacterisation/)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Thank you!

::::: {style="display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;"}
::: {style="flex: 1;"}
### Resources

👉 [Package website](https://ohdsi.github.io/OmopSketch/)\
👉 [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=OmopSketch)\
👉 [PDF manual](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/OmopSketch/OmopSketch.pdf)\
📧 <a href="mailto:cecilia.campanile@ndorms.ox.ac.uk">cecilia.campanile\@ndorms.ox.ac.uk</a>
:::

::: {style="flex: 1; text-align: center;"}
<img src="https://github.com/OHDSI/OmopSketch/blob/main/man/figures/logo.png?raw=true" width="500"/>
:::
:::::
